content:
Every Enterprise Angular Application Eventually Discovers Inheritance

4 min read
19 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

A team starts with a simple component.
export class CustomerComponent {}Everything is clear.
Everything is obvious.
Then another component appears.
Some logic looks similar.
A developer suggests:
Let’s move it into a BaseComponent.
The idea sounds reasonable.
The duplication disappears.
Everyone feels productive.
At first.
Not a Member? Read for FREE here.
The First Base Component
The original goal is usually small.
Example:
export abstract class BaseComponent {destroy$ = new Subject<void>();
ngOnDestroy() {
this.destroy$.next();
this.destroy$.complete();
}
}
Looks harmless.
Looks useful.
Looks reusable.
And often it is.
